title: Household Items - Art History
rationale: craft glue, scissors, , art book
note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:	
- rock (to crush charcoal) (T1)
- disposable plates, bowls or cups (to mix pigments on/in and to hold flour/water mixture) (T1)
- sticks (T1)
- concrete outside (in some form to work on) (T1)
- different types and colors of paper (can be scraps) (T1)
- construction paper including a 12x18 piece of black construction paper (T1/3)
- color pencils, crayons, markers or tempera sticks (any of these will do for adding color) (T1/2/3)
- gallon sized ziplock bags (at least 2 per student) (T1)
- brown paper bag or brown craft paper (T1)

title: Colored Reading Strips
rationale: Helpful for people with a variety of visual processing differences. This multipack provides different colors to experiment with. Once a helpful color has been identified, it can be useful to get several in that color to use as bookmarks.
note: The color is not merely for interest and 2-4 very different colors should be tried before deciding whether they are helpful or not.  Children can often identify for themselves the most helpful color, but teachers can have them read aloud for a simple assessment of whether accuracy and ease are improved.
